label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is wrong according to the news provided. The options are A through D. Let me go through each one carefully based on the text. First, the article says that President Bush stated, "America's space program will go on," so that means they aren't giving up the program because of the accident. So option A says they will give up the space program. That's probably incorrect because the article says the opposite. So A is likely the wrong statement. Option B says NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster. The text mentions that NASA launched an investigation and that it was too early to nail down a precise cause. So B is correct according to the news, so it's not the wrong one. Option C states that before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded during landing. The article says that take-off and re-entry are the most dangerous parts, and in 42 years of US human space flight, there had never been an accident during descent or landing before. The Challenger exploded on take-off, and Columbia broke up during re-entry. So C is correct because before Columbia, no shuttle had exploded during landing or descent.
